subject: Auto-rebase: reduce merge conflicts from parallel agents
instructions:
Reduce merge conflicts when multiple agents work on the same repo in parallel.

## Layer 1: Rebase-before-push in agent prompt
In server/dispatcher.ts (lines ~141-153), update the agent prompt instructions to include a rebase step before pushing:
1. Stage and commit all changes with clear commit messages
2. Before pushing, rebase on the latest base branch to avoid merge conflicts:
   git fetch origin && git rebase origin/{defaultBranch}
   If the rebase has conflicts, resolve them before continuing.
3. Push the branch
4. Create a pull request
5. Output the PR URL

## Layer 2: Auto-update conflicting PR branches

### 2a. Add fields to Ticket type (src/types.ts)
Add to the Ticket interface near hasConflict/conflictDetectedAt:
- rebaseAttemptedAt?: number
- rebaseCount?: number

### 2b. Add autoUpdatePrBranch function (server/dispatcher.ts)
New function near the conflict detection code (~line 554). Uses `gh api --method PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/update-branch` to merge main into the PR branch (equivalent to clicking "Update branch" on GitHub).

Guards:
- Requires ticket.prUrl and ticket.prNumber
- Extracts owner/repo from project.remoteUrl (skip if not set)
- Max 3 attempts (rebaseCount >= 3 -> stop)
- 5-minute cooldown between attempts (rebaseAttemptedAt)
- On API failure (real file-level conflict), increment counter and fall back to current behavior

### 2c. Wire into checkPrStatus (server/dispatcher.ts, ~line 536)
When isConflicting && !ticket.hasConflict (newly detected conflict), call autoUpdatePrBranch(ticket) before setting the conflict flag. This triggers on the 3s dispatcher tick.

### 2d. Post-merge cascade (server/dispatcher.ts, checkAutoMerge ~line 597)
After a successful auto-merge, immediately re-check sibling in_review PRs for the same project to trigger cascade updates.

## Verification
1. npm run build - no type errors
2. Check dispatcher logs for [auto-rebase] messages
